items.title: Capital i Looks Like Lowercase L
items.summary: One of the most profitable gimmicks to come along in a…
items.content_html:
One of the most profitable gimmicks to come along in a bit is the predictions markets.

Betting on today's high in local weather.

Seriously.

And it turns out, one of the very best ones sort of faked their success.

Or at least, the success of its users.

A fake it till you make it situation.

And it got me to thinking.

Uh oh.

If gambling sites have to fake it to make it, what does that mean?

Gambling it would seem would be the least necessary to do anything other than say "Give us money, we'll give you odds."

If you have to fake a winning, even though you're legit, what the heck is going on?

And you know what I came up with?

I don't know if it's a logical conclusion but here it is.

Everything is marketing.

Even bad news.

Even controversy.

Pay the fine and move on.

Bigger than ever.

Worth it.

And also, everyone is selling you something.

Even the free sample person at a warehouse store.

That sheet is free with a string.

Even your friends and fam.

"If you join as a distributor, you get an even bigger discount!"

It doesn't mean selling is a bad thing, which I suppose I sometimes still believe.

My idea of selling is to just be me (sometimes turned to 11) and let people ask to buy.

If you get me, you get it.

No understand me, no understand it.

No big whoop.

But that is selling.

Because we always are.

Except me.

NFS.

items.title: Sunset's Faster
items.summary: I want to say a lot of things today. I really…
items.content_html:
I want to say a lot of things today.

I really do.

But I won't.

Because I don't want to.

Make sense?

No, I suppose it doesn't.

Summer's started.

But it was already sorta here.

June's like that.

Starting.

But already here.

Historically, it's a time to sit back.

Go on vacation.

Other than love, character, and faith, I think the most important thing left to teach is about money.

Defense of self and others, too, I suppose that protects all of the above.

I once heard this standup comedian talk about his horrible credit.

He went on and on about it.

About his credit report.

How he got a physical copy of it.

And how bad it was.

How it didn't stop.

Just went on and on.

And how he didn't care.

"What they gonna do, add a page?"

Shoot.

That had me rolling.

Thank God for wisdom.

But man, that sheet was funny.

items.title: Make My Day
items.summary: Today's my day. As far as days go. Every day is.
items.content_html:
Today's my day.

As far as days go.

Every day is.

My day, that is.

More so today?

Some would like us to think so.

I didn't always get to celebrate today.

At least, not as the center of it.

But I have now for quite some time.

And here's the thing.

As for most holidays and "special occasions."

Every day is my birthday.

You ARE old.

Every day is Father's Day.

Every day is a holiday.

Special.

It just so happens that my loved ones find today a cause to celebrate.

And who am I to say no to a party?

Within reason.

Father is one of my favorite titles.

Might be the most, but I don't want the other titles to feel bad.

I have to do that now, as a father, you see.

No favorites.

All favorites.

Tied for first, certainly.

It's such an important title they made a stationary card day dedicated to honoring it.

Just like moms the month before.

Mamas come first.

As it should be.

And so, today's my day.

Has been for a while, now.

Just like every other day.

So very special.

Thank you.

Now, let's party!!!

Within reason.

Get down off that couch.

Got dang too loud.

Turn that lower.

You ok?

What was that?

Is that a bag of almond M&M's?

For me?

Re-sealable?

Woo-hoo!

My kinda party.

items.title: Save The World
items.summary: 🎵 "We are the world. "We are the children." 🎶 When…
items.content_html:
🎵 "We are the world.

"We are the children." 🎶

When you're a beginner, you want to get right to the heart of the matter.

You have no chill.

Just jumping to conclusions.

Leaping to them.

When you should just chill.

This is a frame that applies to most everything in life.

And it will serve you well if you heed.

If you heed.

Chill.

Relax.

Patience.

You want the steak dinner, you got to have salad first.

Maybe some bread.

Soup.

A tasty beverage.

Small talk.

What are you talking about? You only eat steak!

Grandmaster can break all rules.

That's why he make rules.

For the exceptions.

One of the wisest boxing instructors I learned from talked about rhythm.

How important it is.

And how you have to learn it.

How you have to master it.

1-2-3.

1-2-3.

1-2-3.

Art is dance.

It's martial dancing, really.

And, how you spend years learning the rhythmic patterns.

But, and this is a big but as far as buts are concerned, you only become a true master when you learn to break rhythm.

Mastery by learning the basics, perfecting them, and then interweaving the exact opposite techniques.

1-3-2.

3-1-2.

8-6-7-5-3-0-9.

You break the dance.

You know who's unpredictable?

Who never had rhythm?

The beginner.

The noob.

I always thought that kind of funny.

You spend years mastering something.

Having it down to a science.

But then, having become a bit too predictable, you have to sort of channel your inner noob.

To go back to the beginning.

Where it all started.

To truly get to the heart of the master.

🌉

🎵 "Save the world

We never wanted.

We never wanted

To save the world.

We never wanted

To have to save ourselves." 🎶

🎸

No masters here, though.

Only grands.

Pianos that is.

That I tickle in my mind.

Tickle in my mind.

Tee hee.

Tee hee.

But I repeat myself.

Save yourself.

Save the world.

But I repeat myself.

🐺

items.title: Proof of Future
items.summary: As an old(ish) man who plays the long game, it's hard…
items.content_html:
As an old(ish) man who plays the long game, it's hard to plan for such things in the modern world, particularly so in technology.

Like, say you want to future proof your tech situation.

Your ecosystem.

Get ahead of things.

You know what the term "future proof" gets you in tech?

About five to seven years.

Tops.

What do I mean?

Say you want to buy a laptop.

But you don't buy the base model.

You want it to be relevant and capable for a long time.

You want to get ahead of all the base heads.

I'm talking about bass!

So you start clicking upgrades.

Like a fiend.

More cores.

More RAM.

More storage.

Ouch, ouch, ouch!

Triple ouch.

Thanks, AI.

But, you reason, it'll be future proof!

Redundancy is a good plan for future proofing, but it doesn't stack in tech.

You'll just have more outdated crap.

Eventually.

So tech is disposable.

Like toilet paper.

Also a tech, back in its day.

You mean I don't have to use tree leaves anymore?

Utility off the charts.

But disposable.

Lots of things are like that.

All of which begs the question:

What isn't?

What is really non-disposable?

What is truly future proof?

While you're pondering that, you might want to lock in a laptop.

For the next 5 to 7.

💻

Them data centers aren't going to build themselves.

Are they!??!?!

items.title: Like Caine In Kung Fu
items.summary: Words are important. And as some of you may know, one…
items.content_html:
Words are important.

And as some of you may know, one of my favorite words is actualization.

I usually capitalize it.

Actualization.

It's a big word.

But it has BIG meaning.

Several, in fact, as the best words do.

The one I like best is that you take this idea and turn it into reality.

Through action.

What once wasn't real is now so.

Creating truth.

Of course, the best known definition is the process of achieving one's fullest potential.

Although we usually throw a "self" in front of it.

But I think actualization is more than just the self.

It's about going beyond self.

Sure, sure, be all that you can be.

Spend a whole life doing that. 

But then what?

Then what?

And as selfish as I am, have been, will be, this is my life's mission.

"What happened here is a miracle. And I want you to bleepin' acknowledge it!"

To drop the self.

To then what.

I'm not saying I have achieved actualization.

"From here on in, consider my arse retired."

With or without the self.

"You read the Bible, Ringo?"

"Not regularly, no."

But I'm trying, Ringo.

I'm trying.

Real.

Hard.

Man, you haven't tried at all!

To be the shepherd.

Go.

🥋

"What is a miracle, Vincent?"

"Act of God."

"And what's an Act of God?"

"When, umm, God makes the impossible possible. But this morning, I don't think, qualifies."

items.title: Unalienable
items.summary: We're a young(ish) nation. As far as human history goes. But…
items.content_html:
We're a young(ish) nation.

As far as human history goes.

But we're working on a time capsule.

Did you know that?

America is.

I mean, it's done.

I'm not directly (or indirectly) involved at all.

But I am an American.

So I kinda am.

Involved.

Using the constitutional republic "We" here.

So I'm kinda, technically, adjacently, taking credit for it.

(Unless it fails, then I don't know what you're talking about).

Even though I did nothing.

Boy, does that sound familiar.

Make sense?

It's to be buried on July 4th this year.

Only to be opened 250 years thereafter.

On the five hunny.

On the five Benjamins.

Want to know what's in it?

Some cool and weird stuff.

Like an iPhone 17 Pro Max.

Why are we putting a device made in China and/or India in our USA time capsule?

Shhhhhhh.

🤫

Shhhhhh.

Shhh!

Zip it.

Where was I?

Coca-Cola (sadly not Coke Zero) glass bottle.

Sports mem.

Synthetic DNA (including of Abe Lincoln's hand. Just his hand. Seriously).

You know, stuff.

All 50 states get to represent.

DC, too.

They put in rocks.

Poems.

Music.

Coins.

Qubit chip.

And, perhaps most importantly, copies of our founding documents.

Mmmm, words.

Containing self-evident truths that seem to require an explanation these days.

Hopefully not in two fiddy.

Hopefully.

Life.

Liberty.

The pursuit (not the guarantee) of happiness.

We can't take them away.

Unless we have to or want to.

Then we will.

No biggie.

Someone once said America is the first country to be founded on a good idea.

And I always liked that.

I don't know if we're the first.

But I like the last part.

Founded on a good idea.

To two and half centuries.

And another.

And so on (but we should save that for the millennium capsule toasters).

We hold these truths.

On our sacred Honor.

🇺🇸

items.title: Ever Laugh And Fart At The Same Time?
items.summary: Alright. I admit it. I've seen those videos where someone will…
items.content_html:
Alright.

I admit it.

I've seen those videos where someone will test the generosity of a person.

Ask 'em for a dollar or something.

Get rejected a hundred times.

Find one who gleefully gives it.

Person filming takes it.

Walks away.

Comes back dramatically.

Gives the money back.

And then gifts them an iPhone.

Or something.

The tears flow.

Trumpets blare.

But here's the weird part.

A lot of these videos don't end there.

They don't end at the elation of ecosystems.

You would think you cut it here.

But no.

The giver then says something like, "If you could tell the world one thing, what would you say?"

And I always think, what a terrible thing to ask someone.

Right on the spot.

Right then and there.

Off the top of their elated inflated heads.

So I've been giving out a lot of dollars lately.

No, you haven't.

To anyone who asks, really.

Stop lying.

Not in hopes of getting a dollar free iPhone.

We call that a fringe benefit.

But for my moment.

My moment.

"Could I have a dollar?"

Oh snap.

Of course!!!

Here it comes.

"If you could tell the world one thing, what would it be?"

[Nervous toot].

Oh no.

Can we do a retake?

Hey!

Aww crap.

📱 

Sweet.

items.title: Unctuous
items.summary: Well, hello. How are you? Have you eaten? Come, sit down…
items.content_html:
Well, hello.

How are you?

Have you eaten?

Come, sit down and join us.

We were just about to dine on some home cooked Korean food.

Prepared by yours truly.

The best cuisine on the planet.

Would you do us the honor of having a little less food to throw out?

Yeah, pretty sure you have never said that in your life.

Oh bother.

It's good to have an imagination.

Yes?

💭

Stuffing our grocery holes is a term I heard someone from Maine use to refer to the art of consumption.

Stuffing our grocery holes.

That just tickles me.

So I use it from time to time.

To mixed reception.

(My sweet spot).

I don't have a point today.

Like many days.

I'm just here. 

Taking it all in.

I am simply amused with myself.

You got to be your number one fan.

You got to be your number one fan.

Woah.

Right?

Clear eyes.

Full belly.

Always win.

After nap.

Win after nap.

Plenty of time to win.

After nap.

💤

items.title: Long Gamer
items.summary: How does a person, who almost passed, still play the long…
items.content_html:
How does a person, who almost passed, still play the long game?

That's a good question.

One I'm dancing with.

Every.

Single.

Day.

So far, it's been to slow down.

Smell the tulips.

Wiggle toes in carpet, grass, water, and sand.

And immerse myself in my family's frenzied, kinetic, jubilance.

And to look skyward.

Heavenward.

And smile.

You would think there is a sense of urgency here.

As every second goes.

But that's short thinking.

Instant gratification.

Everyone thinks this way.

And I kinda see why now.

Now!!!

But that's not me.

Hasn't been me in a while.

In ages.

I believe in planned spontaneity.

In asymmetric bets.

In an apex opus.

No all-ins, though.

Not any more.

I think in generations, still.

Future, though.

Always future.

Perhaps a weakness.

Certainly a strength.

I don't think I've ever needed much.

Never wanted much.

Some might disagree.

Peace is an interesting concept.

I have achieved it.

Contrary to what you read here.

This is just nonsense.

But it's mine.

Of course I speak only of the material in this context of limited want.

Spirituality is an infinite want.

An infinite need.

You gotta have faith a faith a faitha.

You gotta have faith.

Bay Beeah!!

👢e

My body healing; my soul larger than ever.

These are attributes I hope to pass on.

When I do.

Before and after, too.

🦁
